justifications.completeness: Only 1 of 4 ground-truth pedestrians and 3 of 5 ground-truth vehicles are reported, so many actors are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes diverse attributes (types, trunk/door state, age, descriptions) but misses key ground-truth motion labels and object identities.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some useful spatial predicates are provided (truck blocking road, relative positions) but relations are incomplete and potentially imprecise for several objects.
justifications.overall: The output is partially detailed but incomplete and contains important omissions and incorrect motion labeling, yielding a below-average result.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth objects are missing: only one of two pedestrians is reported and most vehicles are omitted, with at least one vehicle motion status incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes multiple semantic attributes (actions, age, vehicle types, door/trunk status, spatial notes) but omits several ground-truth objects and contains some incorrect labels, reducing overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are plausible for the listed objects but are incomplete, inconsistently applied, and cannot be fully validated against the ground truth which lacks explicit spatial annotations.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers useful details but is significantly incomplete and has key attribute errors, resulting in moderate-low overall quality for driving-scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Captured a few entities but missed most pedestrians and several vehicles from the ground truth, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes multiple attribute types (actions, age, vehicle type, motion status, door/trunk) showing richness per reported object, but this is limited to few objects and contains some incorrect labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides plausible spatial relations for reported entities, but many ground-truth objects lack any spatial annotation and some relations/motion statuses appear inconsistent.
justifications.overall: Partially useful extraction with decent attribute detail for reported items but overall low coverage and some incorrect/missing labels reduce its reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians but only two of four ground-truth vehicles, recovering 4 of 6 annotated objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute labels (actions, age, vehicle type, motion, doors/trunk, spatial notes) but lacks object IDs and mislabels at least one vehicle motion compared to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes useful spatial relations like ahead of ego and near store, but these relations are not present in the ground truth for verification and some spatial assertions conflict with vehicle motion labels.
justifications.overall: A moderately informative extraction that captures key pedestrian details and some vehicles with rich attributes, but misses multiple vehicles and contains motion-label inconsistencies reducing overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 3 pedestrians and 3 of 5 vehicles and also misclassified pedestrian actions, so several objects and labels are missing or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides many attribute fields such as age, trunk and door states, and spatial relations, but key semantic labels like pedestrian actions are incorrect and two parked vehicles are omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are present and partially useful (near storefront, behind/ahead), but they do not match the ground truth simple contexts for pedestrians and miss relations for the omitted parked vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures several object types and attributes but has notable omissions and incorrect pedestrian actions leading to only moderate reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only 2 of 4 pedestrians and 4 of 6 vehicles and misses key annotations (e.g., one pedestrian carrying an item), so many ground-truth entities are absent.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes useful attributes like type, motion_status, and age but omits several object-specific labels and finer context (e.g., transporting for a pedestrian and multiple parked vehicle statuses), yielding moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some relative cues (near, behind, ahead) are provided and helpful, but relations are sparse, not consistently aligned with ground-truth coverage, and sometimes imprecise for context-critical judgments.
justifications.overall: Because the extraction misses multiple actors and important attributes while providing only partial and sometimes imprecise spatial cues, it is partially useful but insufficient for reliable scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures 5 of 9 ground-truth entities (2/3 pedestrians and 3/6 vehicles) and thus misses a substantial portion of the scene.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides many attribute types (actions, contexts, age, vehicle type, motion, trunk/doors) adding depth beyond the ground truth, though some action labels are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for all detected entities and are generally plausible, but they are somewhat coarse and several scene objects are missing so the spatial coverage is incomplete.
justifications.overall: The extraction is moderately useful due to good semantic detail and plausible spatial descriptions but reduced overall utility from missed entities and a few incorrect labels.

justifications.completeness: Covers 3 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and 3 of 4 vehicles, missing two people and one vehicle so coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (communicative, transporting, trunk/door status, age) beyond base labels but several attributes are incorrect or mismatched to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible spatial relations but they are somewhat vague and some positional/motion statements conflict with ground truth (e.g., moving vehicles labelled stopped).
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: reasonable attribute richness but only partial detection coverage and several important semantic and motion misclassifications reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures some pedestrians and two vehicles but misses multiple ground-truth persons and vehicles and fails to report key labels like jaywalking and several communicative tags.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides many attribute fields (age, transporting, doors/trunk) but contains incorrect values (e.g., pulling) and omits or mislabels important semantics such as talking in group and jaywalking for several people.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several reasonable spatial relations are provided (sidewalk, middle of road, left side) but they are incomplete and do not consistently capture critical positions for all ground-truth jaywalking pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is partially correct and somewhat informative but incomplete and with notable semantic and instance-level errors that reduce its reliability for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Many ground truth objects and attributes are missing or mislabeled, for example two vehicles are not reported and jaywalking contexts are not captured.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes useful details like clothing and atomic actions but omits critical contextual labels (jaywalking) and fails to report several vehicle states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some coarse spatial relations are provided but they are inconsistent with the ground truth jaywalking positions and several vehicle spatial relations are absent due to missing vehicles.
justifications.overall: The output contains partially correct pedestrian actions and some object attributes but misses multiple objects and key contextual labels, resulting in low overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction fails to match several ground-truth entities and contexts (4 GT persons vs 6 extracted with misassigned contexts and 3 GT vehicles vs only 1 extracted), so many objects are missing or incorrectly reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many semantic fields (actions, transporting, age, vehicle states) beyond the ground truth, but several asserted attributes (e.g., carrying) are unsupported or incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Although multiple spatial relations are provided, several contradict the ground truth (persons who are jaywalking or on the road are labeled as on sidewalks), indicating poor spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: The output is semantically rich but suffers from missing vehicles, incorrect pedestrian contexts and unreliable spatial relations, yielding low overall usefulness for safety-critical perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures several pedestrians but misses key ground truth items such as the two jaywalking pedestrians and two moving vehicles while introducing an extra pedestrian, so many ground truth entities and critical labels are omitted or mismatched.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides detailed attributes like clothing, age, and object types but fails to report crucial semantic labels from the ground truth such as jaywalking and vehicle motion status, reducing its actionable richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reported spatial relations mostly describe storefront proximity and sidewalk placement, which contradicts ground truth indications that several pedestrians are on the road jaywalking, so spatial placement is often incorrect.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction contains useful but noisy and incomplete information, missing critical motion and context cues and misplacing pedestrians, resulting in limited utility for driving safety decisions.

justifications.completeness: Detected all six pedestrians but missed two vehicles and misclassified some pedestrian contexts and a transporting attribute.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute types (actions, context, age, transport) but includes incorrect transporting information, omits jaywalking labels present in the GT, and fails to report multiple vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several spatial relations which add value but they cannot be fully validated against the GT (no spatial annotations) and some relations appear imprecise.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: pedestrian presence is captured but semantic errors and missing vehicle detections reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction finds most objects (3 of 4 pedestrians and 2 vehicles) and many attributes but misses one pedestrian and mislabels at least one pedestrian/vehicle motion status so it is incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides many semantic fields (actions, contexts, age, trunk/door states, spatial relations) but contains some incorrect labels (a pedestrian marked standing and a vehicle marked stopped) reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are generally present and plausible (right/left/near/store/ahead) but are incomplete due to the missing pedestrian and there is limited precision in relation phrasing.
justifications.overall: The extraction is fairly detailed and useful but missing one person and containing a few incorrect action/motion labels, resulting in moderate overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Detected all four pedestrians but mislocalized/misclassified some and detected only one of three vehicles, causing moderate incompleteness.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (actions, age, transport, door/trunk states) but misses jaywalking labels and mislabels at least one pedestrian's pose.
justifications.spatial_relations: Supplies spatial relations for most agents but contains inaccuracies (sidewalk vs road, missing vehicle relations) leading to partial correctness.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: good semantic coverage but several localization and classification errors and missing vehicles reduce reliability for driving decisions.

justifications.completeness: Most pedestrians are detected (6) but one vehicle is missing and at least two pedestrians have incorrect context (on-road vs sidewalk) so the extraction is incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides many attributes (atomic action, age, communicative, transporting, spatial relations and vehicle door/trunk state) offering good semantic detail despite some incorrect labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for every pedestrian but several are incorrect (two GT pedestrians are on the road while the extraction places them on sidewalks) and one vehicle relation/instance is missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely informative and detailed but contains notable omissions (one vehicle) and several spatial/contextual errors that reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected all four pedestrians but missed one of two vehicles (5/6 entities matched).
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed attributes (actions, context, age, vehicle states) and even extra scene context, capturing most semantic fields.
justifications.spatial_relations: Lists multiple spatial relations but contains at least one clear contradiction (person ahead of truck vs truck ahead of person) and several relations are unverifiable, reducing reliability.
justifications.overall: Good coverage of pedestrians and rich semantic detail, but missing a vehicle and containing contradictory spatial relations lowers overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 6 of 8 ground-truth pedestrians (missed two) and correctly reported the single vehicle, so partial coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided many attribute fields (actions, context, age) but introduced an incorrect transporting label and missed the jaywalking context from ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Included left/right/center relations for most detected people and the vehicle but omitted relations for the two missed pedestrians and did not mark the jaywalking location.
justifications.overall: Extraction is partially accurate with correct vehicle attributes and many correct person attributes but incomplete detection and a notable false transporting label reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected all five pedestrians but misclassified one pedestrian as standing and added an incorrect carrying label while missing a parked vehicle, so coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes beyond the ground truth such as age and transporting, increasing richness, but some attribute assignments are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes useful spatial relations and relative vehicle placement that appear partly accurate, but some relations are imprecise and cannot be fully validated against the provided ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction is informative and semantically rich but contains notable inaccuracies in actions and attributes and omits a parked vehicle, resulting in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses one of two annotated persons (the bicyclist) and two of five annotated vehicles, so overall object coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction supplies many semantic attributes (actions, age, motion_status, trunk/doors, spatial tags) but omits or misrepresents important context like jaywalking and the bicyclist and may misclassify a van as a generic car.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are reasonable and accurate for the reported objects (left/right/behind/near construction) but are sparse and absent for the missing objects.
justifications.overall: The extraction is moderately semantically rich and often correct for detected objects but is significantly incomplete in object coverage and misses critical contextual details.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two pedestrians and four of five vehicles, missing a bicyclist and a stopped vehicle, so coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attribute fields (actions, motion, trunk/doors, age) but some attributes are incorrect or missing (missed biking/jaywalking and mislabelled a stopped vehicle), so moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides spatial relations for objects and most parked vehicles are located, but the pedestrian's context and the stopped vehicle's status/position are incorrect, reducing accuracy.
justifications.overall: Partially accurate extraction with useful attribute reporting but misses key actors and some critical semantic errors, yielding a moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction finds both pedestrians and one parked vehicle but misses at least one parked and one moving vehicle from the ground truth and omits the biking actor's correct action.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output populates many attribute fields (age, communicative, transporting) but contains incorrect or incomplete semantic labels for key behaviors such as biking versus walking and jaywalking context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are generic and partly incorrect (e.g., parked on the left sidewalk) and do not capture the jaywalking spatial context of the walking person.
justifications.overall: The extraction has useful structure and some correct facts but misses multiple objects and mislabels important actions and spatial context, reducing reliability for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two of four annotated vehicles and mischaracterized one pedestrian (failed to identify the bicyclist), so many objects and key attributes are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides many attribute fields (age, door/trunk states) but mislabels actions and omits important contextual labels like biking and jaywalking, reducing semantic correctness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided for pedestrians and vehicles, but they are incomplete and partially inconsistent with the ground truth and scene cues.
justifications.overall: Partially informative extraction with several correct attributes but multiple omissions and incorrect action/context labels, limiting its usefulness for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected both people but misclassified one as walking instead of biking and found only one of two vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes like age and object states but some labels are incorrect or inconsistent such as vehicle type and the action of the bicyclist.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives relative locations for pedestrians and the vehicle which are partially correct but lacks precise relation for the bicyclist and misses the parked vehicle context.
justifications.overall: Partial coverage with reasonable attribute richness but notable classification errors and a missed vehicle reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses the moving vehicle and several parked vehicles and misidentifies one pedestrian's action, covering only a subset of ground-truth objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attribute types (age, motion, doors) but contains incorrect action labels and omits several vehicle attributes from ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives explicit relative placements for detected entities but some relations contradict ground truth (e.g., pedestrian on sidewalk vs on road) and many objects lack relations due to being missing.
justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat detailed extraction but with notable omissions and errors in actions and vehicle detection, yielding moderate-low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses multiple ground-truth vehicles and adds an extra pedestrian, so overall object coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM supplies many attribute fields (age, motion_status, trunk/doors, contextual labels) but key labels are incorrect or omitted for some objects, yielding moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several spatial relations (cyclist on the road, van ahead) are correct, but spurious relations and missing relations for several GT vehicles reduce accuracy.
justifications.overall: Given missed detections, a false positive pedestrian, and some incorrect attribute assignments, the extraction quality is limited though partially informative.

justifications.completeness: Missing or misclassified key ground-truth entities (the cyclist is treated as a vehicle and several parked vehicles are omitted), so many ground-truth objects are absent or incorrectly labeled.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides many attribute fields (age, communicative, transporting, trunk/doors, motion status) offering richer semantics than the ground truth, but several attributes are incorrect or assigned to the wrong entities.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations like left/right/near/ahead are present and generally useful, but some relations stem from misclassification (e.g., cyclist treated as vehicle) and lack finer precision.
justifications.overall: Partially informative with useful attributes and relations but significant misclassification and missing objects meaning the extraction is unreliable for full scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians and two vehicles but missed several vehicles and misidentified some actions and contexts.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attribute fields (age, actions, transport, door and trunk states) demonstrating richness but several semantic labels are incorrect or inconsistent with ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides explicit spatial relations for agents and objects but some relations contradict ground truth context, for example labeling a walking person as on the sidewalk instead of on the road.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance with reasonable attribute coverage but notable errors in action labeling and missing vehicle instances reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses multiple parked vehicles from the ground truth and fails to label the person's atomic action (sitting).
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes (scene context, object types, motion status, trunk/door state, age) but includes unsupported assertions (closed trunk/doors) and misses the atomic action.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives accurate and relevant spatial relations for the person, truck, and parked bicycles that match the scene.
justifications.overall: Contains useful semantic details and correct spatial relations but is incomplete and makes some incorrect or unsupported attribute claims.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures the cyclist and a truck but misses multiple parked vehicles from the ground truth and incorrectly omits the annotated atomic action for the person.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many attribute fields (age, motion_status, trunk/doors, spatial relations) but some key attributes are incorrect or inapplicable such as the person's atomic action and the truck trunk/door status.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are reasonable (cyclist on road, truck ahead of cyclist) but the relations are incomplete relative to the missing parked vehicles and lack finer context.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially useful with several correct labels and many provided attributes, but it contains notable omissions and some incorrect attribute assignments reducing its reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected the single cyclist but only two vehicles were reported versus five in ground truth, missing multiple parked vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes like motion and trunk/door states but mislabels or omits key person action (sitting) and lacks attributes for many vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives coarse relations for the cyclist and two vehicles that are plausible but does not describe spatial relations for the majority of ground truth vehicles.
justifications.overall: Partially correct scene understanding with meaningful attributes for detected objects but incomplete detection and some incorrect or missing semantics lower the overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Misses multiple parked vehicles listed in the ground truth and mislabels the pedestrian's atomic action, so the extraction is substantially incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (type, motion_status, trunk/doors, spatial relations, age) indicating high richness despite some incorrect or unsupported values.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers plausible spatial relations for the bicycle and truck but does not align with ground-truth object coverage and omits relations for many vehicles.
justifications.overall: Partially informative with useful semantic detail but key omissions and an incorrect pedestrian action reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only five pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth contains seven persons and one vehicle, missing several actors and some key behaviors such as pushing and phone use.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple attribute fields (age, transporting, communicative, etc.) and some fine details but contains incorrect or unverified assignments (e.g., missing pushing, mislabeling the traffic officer and some contexts).
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are reasonably detailed (left/right/near/behind) but include unsupported claims and lack alignment with ground-truth labels like jaywalking or explicit vehicle relation semantics.
justifications.overall: The output captures several useful semantic elements and spatial cues but has significant omissions and mismatches to the ground truth, yielding only moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected most pedestrians but missed at least one person and all vehicles and several transport and communicative labels.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields but misses key attributes like multiple transporting and communicative behaviors, omits all vehicle information, and introduces unsupported labels such as traffic directing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several relative descriptions but multiple relations are incorrect or unsupported by the ground truth and important relations like jaywalking positions are not represented.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is partially informative but incomplete and contains incorrect semantic and spatial claims, limiting its reliability for safe driving decisions.

justifications.completeness: The VLM found most pedestrians but missed two vehicles from the ground truth and failed to capture several important person attributes like pushing and looking-into-phone.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (age, transporting, action) and one correct transporting label, but many fields are set to none and key contextual labels such as crossing and communicative behaviors were missed.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for many people but are coarse, sometimes vague, and the extraction omits explicit relations present in the ground truth like crossing at a pedestrian crossing and several vehicle placements.
justifications.overall: The output captures a fair portion of entities and some attributes but misses several vehicles and important contextual and communicative labels, yielding a moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 pedestrians and 1 vehicle versus 6 annotated persons and 2 vehicles, missing multiple actors and adding an extra traffic officer.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captured many attributes like actions, transporting and spatial relations but missed communicative cues and several complex contexts such as jaywalking and looking into phone.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided useful proximity and side-of-road relations but failed to mark jaywalking and some relative relations to crossings and omitted one vehicle location.
justifications.overall: Partial match: moderate semantic detail but incomplete detection and missing safety-critical labels lead to a middling overall score.

justifications.completeness: Extraction detects 5 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while ground truth lists 7 persons and 3 vehicles, so several entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes many semantic fields but misses or mislabels important attributes such as transporting actions (carrying, pushing), communicative cues, and illegal crossing context, reducing correctness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Extraction provides several spatial relations that appear speculative or unsupported by the ground truth annotations, for example a traffic officer and holding hands are not present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Given the number of missing entities, incorrect contexts, and speculative spatial relations the extraction is incomplete and of limited reliability for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: Detected 6 of the 7 ground truth pedestrians and only 1 of 2 vehicles, missing the bicyclist and failing to capture several correct action or transporting labels.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies many semantic fields per entity (actions, context, transporting, age, spatial relations) offering high richness despite multiple incorrect or inconsistent values.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some relations like near construction barrier and left sidewalk are captured correctly, but on-road versus sidewalk placements and pairwise relations are often incorrect or incomplete.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall quality with good attribute coverage but notable omissions and several incorrect action and transport annotations that reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only 6 pedestrians and 1 vehicle compared to 8 persons and 4 vehicles in the ground truth, missing several actors (including a bicyclist and seated person) and many vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction contains many semantic fields (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) but several attribute assignments conflict with the ground truth and there is no mapping to track IDs to resolve correspondences.
justifications.spatial_relations: Per-person spatial phrases are present but are vague, sometimes contradict the ground-truth simple contexts (sidewalk vs road), and lack precise relations to specific vehicles or scene landmarks.
justifications.overall: The output provides useful semantic detail but is notably incomplete and spatially inconsistent, limiting its reliability for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists more pedestrian instances but fails to match ground-truth identities and omits or misreports several key objects and vehicle instances and attributes (e.g., moving vehicle, bicyclist, pushing transport).
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many attribute fields (actions, context, age, spatial relations) but often leaves communicative and transporting as none or misses specific labels present in the ground truth such as pushing and looking into phone.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several spatial cues are correctly noted (sidewalk, near traffic controller, parked on right) but there are notable misassignments between on-road versus on-sidewalk and missing relations for multiple vehicles.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures high-level scene elements and some attributes but contains multiple omissions, incorrect assignments, and mismatches with ground-truth object roles and states, limiting its reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected a majority of visible pedestrians and some vehicles but missed multiple ground-truth persons (including a bicyclist) and two vehicles and omitted several entity-level attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Offers many attribute fields but frequently omits or mislabels key semantics such as transporting actions and communicative behaviors that are present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides spatial relations for several detections but contains notable inaccuracies and omissions relative to ground-truth contexts, such as confusing on-road versus on-sidewalk placements.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures basic entities and some relations but has enough missing and incorrect labels to limit its overall usefulness for dependable scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only 3 pedestrians and 2 vehicles versus 7 persons and 4 vehicles in the ground truth, missing multiple actors and many transporting annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many attribute fields (atomic_action, communicative, transporting, age) but contains several incorrect or missing values (e.g., missing carrying/pushing labels, an unannotated traffic officer) reducing its semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial cues are plausible (right/left, near barriers) but they are incomplete and not fully aligned with the ground truth object set, so they are partially useful.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is limited by many missed detections and several incorrect/missing attribute annotations despite moderate semantic detail and some correct spatial cues.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 5 of 8 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles and omitted several actors and key attributes like carrying, pushing and biking.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic labels (walking, age, simple context) but misses important behavior details (transporting, sitting/biking) and includes invented roles such as a 'security guard'.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives coarse spatial cues (left, near building, behind crosswalk) that are partially useful but limited in coverage and not clearly aligned with all ground-truth positions.
justifications.overall: Moderate-low overall: some useful detections and spatial hints but many missing actors, omitted behaviors, and occasional incorrect or unsupported descriptions.

justifications.completeness: Many pedestrians and vehicles present in the ground truth are missing from the extraction and several object attributes are omitted, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes useful fields like actions, age, and some context but omits or misreports important attributes such as transporting details and vehicle states for most objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few spatial relations are provided but they are sparse, somewhat imprecise, and fail to describe many relative positions among the multiple pedestrians and vehicles in the scene.
justifications.overall: Given the low actor coverage, missing transport annotations, and limited spatial detail, the extraction is of limited reliability for autonomous driving perception needs.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth people and vehicles are missing or unrecognized (extraction lists 5 pedestrians vs 6 and 1 vehicle vs 4) and several object attributes like transporting/biking are omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides some attributes (age, motion) and spatial notes but omits important behaviors and contexts like 'pushing' and 'jaywalking', and mislabels vehicle detail, reducing semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are present and plausible (near construction barriers, next to), but they are incomplete, not linked to GT ids, and miss relations for several actors.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures basic scene elements but is incomplete, misses key behaviors and many vehicles, and therefore has limited reliability for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Misses several ground-truth people (including a bicyclist) and most vehicles while adding an extra pedestrian, so coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many per-person attributes (actions, transporting, age, contextual notes) beyond the ground truth but includes some unmatched or extraneous labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes useful local relations (near, pushing on right) and side descriptors but omits several GT positional cues and vehicle relations.
justifications.overall: Moderately informative with decent attribute detail but incomplete and partially mismatched in object coverage and spatial accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 5 persons and 2 of 4 vehicles and omitted key attributes like transporting for two ground-truth pedestrians, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes some useful labels (age, atomic actions) but omits transporting for actors that are pushing and lacks several complex contextual labels, giving only partial semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but multiple pedestrian/vehicle relations are missing or vague and several relations do not align with ground truth, reducing spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures basic scene elements but misses multiple actors, important attributes, and many accurate spatial relations, yielding below average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles, missing a pedestrian who is pushing/getting on a 2-wheel vehicle and one vehicle from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attribute fields (actions, age, transporting, communicative) but omits complex contextual labels and fails to mark transporting for pedestrians that are pushing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides useful spatial relations (near sidewalk, near handcart, directing on road) though some vehicle status/placement (stopped vs parked) and a pedestrian context differ from the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall performance with reasonable attribute extraction and spatial cues but missing key actors and several semantic mismatches reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 pedestrians versus 5 in the ground truth and 2 vehicles versus 3, missing one person with transporting/pushing context and one vehicle, so the extraction is incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (actions, age, transporting) and scene description but omits key contextual labels (pushing/getting on a two‑wheel vehicle), and mislabels or omits some vehicle details, reducing semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations provided (sidewalk, near crates, center of road) are plausible for some subjects but omit on‑road relations for others and lack correspondence to specific ground truth IDs, yielding partial accuracy.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: reasonable attribute coverage and plausible spatial cues but missing objects and important contextual labels lead to only a partial match with the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected the expected number of pedestrians and multiple vehicles but missed or misassigned important attributes like transporting/pushing and some atomic actions.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful extra labels (traffic officer, handcart), scene context and spatial relations but contains incorrect action/context labels and omits transporting details present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Captures many relative positions (near sidewalk, cones, handcart) that align with the scene but misplaces the walking-on-road vs sidewalk relation for at least one person.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall: good entity detection and some richer descriptors, but errors in key action and transporting attributes limit reliability for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: Covers only 3 of 6 ground truth persons and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing many actors and vehicle instances.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attribute labels but omits key semantics present in the ground truth such as pushing, biking, jaywalking, and several contextual annotations while including at least one unsupported transporting label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers spatial relations for the reported actors but many relations are unverified by the annotations and several nearby actors are omitted, reducing overall reliability.
justifications.overall: Extraction is partially informative but notably incomplete and partially inaccurate, limiting its utility for downstream perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Captured most pedestrians (7 entries) including the person pushing a cart, but missed the sitting/biking actor and misclassified several on-road vs side-walk contexts.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes per person (actions, context, transporting, age) yielding high semantic detail, though some critical labels (e.g., biking/sitting) are absent or incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for nearly all pedestrians and the vehicle, but relations are sometimes imprecise, duplicated, or inconsistent with on-road versus sidewalk positions.
justifications.overall: Overall useful and detailed but contains notable omissions and misclassifications (missing biking actor, context errors, duplicate-like entries) that reduce reliability for safety-critical decisions.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only 4 pedestrians out of about 10 in the ground truth and 2 vehicles versus 5 annotated, so recall is low and many actors are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains many semantic fields (actions, transporting, age, communicative) beyond the ground truth, but several attribute assignments are incorrect or speculative.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided relative relations (e.g., near stacked crates, left/right) are plausible for the extracted entities but cover only a subset of actors and are not consistently alignable with ground-truth tracks.
justifications.overall: While semantically informative in places, the extraction has low completeness and some erroneous attribute labels, limiting its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction enumerates seven pedestrians matching the ground truth count but misses or misidentifies most vehicles and their motion states, reducing completeness.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many attribute fields (age, context, communicative) but contains several incorrect or vague labels for atomic actions and bicycling roles, limiting semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are supplied for most pedestrians and one vehicle but are often coarse or inconsistent with the ground truth and many vehicle relations are absent.
justifications.overall: Pedestrian coverage is reasonable but action misclassifications and major omissions for vehicles produce a moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Partial detection: the extraction lists 6 pedestrians versus 7 in the ground truth and only 1 vehicle versus 3, missing multiple actors and some vehicle instances.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction contains a wide range of attributes (actions, transporting, age, trunk/doors) but includes incorrect action labels (misses bicyclist sitting/biking) and some transport errors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Many spatial relations are provided and generally plausible for scene understanding, though some are vague and cannot be fully verified against the ground truth annotations.
justifications.overall: Moderately useful extraction with good semantic coverage but incomplete instance detection and notable mislabelings, especially for vehicle counts and bicyclist actions.

justifications.completeness: Extraction finds multiple pedestrians but misses several ground-truth persons and most vehicles and also adds/misaligns detections, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction supplies many semantic fields (actions, context, age, transporting, spatial relations) but contains important mislabels (bicyclist marked as walking, senior marked as adult) and misses transporting for the carrier.
justifications.spatial_relations: Many spatial relations are provided and generally useful (right/left, near cones/bicycles) but some relations are vague or not aligned with the ground-truth placements.
justifications.overall: Overall the output is moderately detailed but incorrect labels, missing objects, and misassignments limit its reliability for autonomous driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected 5 of 7 pedestrians and the truck, missing at least two people and one contextual action, so coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes (actions, age, transporting) and vehicle state but mislabels a senior as an adult and omits one person's contextual label, reducing semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes explicit left/right and near relations and correctly links the truck to the cones, with generally accurate spatial descriptions for most agents.
justifications.overall: Accurate vehicle annotation and several correct pedestrian descriptions yield useful output, but missing pedestrians and some attribute/context errors limit overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected multiple pedestrians and the parked vehicle but missed or mischaracterized at least one ground-truth person (standing) and introduced an extra pedestrian.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (clothing hints, transporting, communicative, ages) beyond the ground truth but several attribute labels like transporting and age are incorrect or unsupported.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several spatial relations and correctly links the vehicle to nearby construction, but it misplaces pedestrian contexts (sidewalk vs road) and over-associates relations, reducing accuracy.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is informative and rich but contains notable errors in pedestrian counts, actions, ages, and some spatial contexts, yielding moderate reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists four pedestrians and one vehicle but misses one standing person and mislabels the age of a senior, so several ground-truth entities are omitted or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides many semantic attributes (actions, context, transporting, age, vehicle states) matching ground truth fields, but some attribute assignments (age, a missing person) are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several spatial relations are correct (near truck, near construction barriers, on road/sidewalk) but at least one transporting person is assigned to the wrong location and one person is omitted, reducing accuracy.
justifications.overall: The extraction is reasonably detailed and captures many correct attributes but omissions and some incorrect assignments (missing standing person, wrong age and one spatial mismatch) lower the overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 7 ground truth persons and the single vehicle, missing three pedestrians and their annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attributes per person and the vehicle but mislabels age, omits a known transporting action, and misses some contextual labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes relevant relations like near truck and next to cones but spatial relations are incomplete and somewhat vague for several pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance with reasonable attribute coverage for detected objects but missing multiple pedestrians and key semantics, resulting in only partial correctness.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 6 ground-truth persons, missing two individuals including the person talking on phone and thus omitting their annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Most attributes (atomic actions, transporting, age) are correctly labeled, but some simple_contexts differ from ground truth and the important communicative label talking on phone is missed.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several spatial relations are provided and plausible, but they are inconsistent across individuals, missing for the two omitted pedestrians, and sometimes imprecise.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: correct labels for detected persons but incomplete detection and missed communicative cues reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports five pedestrians and the parked truck but omits two ground-truth persons and fails to capture one communicative label (talking on phone).
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes multiple semantic attributes (actions, transporting, age, vehicle states) and correctly identifies one transporting instance but has context mismatches and misses a communicative annotation.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several spatial relations are provided (near truck, in front/behind, near store) which are useful though fairly coarse and not precisely aligned to ground-truth IDs.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall: useful semantic detail and correct vehicle state but missing actors and at least one important communicative label reduce completeness and reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected all five pedestrians and the ground-truth vehicle, though it added an extra spurious truck.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (actions, age, transporting, trunk/door states) beyond the ground truth but several attribute labels are incorrect or inconsistent.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes numerous spatial relations that are partially correct (near truck, near building) but mislabels locations for some pedestrians (sidewalk vs road) and invents a second truck position.
justifications.overall: Extraction is fairly complete and semantically rich but suffers from multiple attribute and spatial errors leading to moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports 3 pedestrians while the ground truth lists 6, so about half of the persons are detected and several contextual roles are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some atomic actions, ages, and simple contexts are provided but many ground-truth labels (e.g., communicative 'talking on phone' and 'exiting a building') are omitted, reducing semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction includes specific spatial relations for each detected person (left/right, near cones, near shop) but these relations are not annotated in the ground truth for verification, so they are plausible but unverified.
justifications.overall: The output captures some correct pedestrian attributes and useful spatial cues but misses half the pedestrians and several important contextual/communicative labels, yielding a below-average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Only two of five ground-truth persons are reported, so the extraction misses the majority of actors in the frame.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies actions, contexts, ages, and additional spatial details for detected pedestrians but omits several actors and a communicative label present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations align with the scene and extra details are provided (near shop), but relations for three missing pedestrians are absent and one asserted relation (between cones) is not corroborated by the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Partial correct attribute capture for two pedestrians combined with missing actors and at least one missed communicative/contextual label yields a moderate overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 5 ground-truth persons and no vehicles, yielding roughly 60% entity coverage while missing at least two annotated pedestrians and their attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields for detected pedestrians but fails to capture important semantics present in the ground truth such as carrying with both hands and talking on the phone.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes left/right and proximity relations for some pedestrians but omits or misrepresents spatial/contextual cues like entering a building and standing from the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall: some correct detections and descriptive fields are present, but several pedestrians and key semantic/contextual labels are missing or incorrect, reducing reliability for driving perception use.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 5 ground-truth persons and missed two individuals while also mis-assigning at least one action, so coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple semantic attributes (actions, context, transporting, age) for detections but mislabels one person's action and misses a communicative label, yielding moderate richness with errors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Supplies plausible relations to the shop and construction barriers for detected pedestrians but omits relations for the missed persons and some relations are vague, so reasonably good but incomplete.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall performance: useful attribute extraction for detected pedestrians but two missing persons and several incorrect or missing attributes reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 6 ground-truth persons and no vehicles, missing several actors and related annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted some attributes like age and transporting but missed or misassigned multiple communicative and contextual labels present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided a few spatial relations but they are sparse and not well aligned with ground-truth contexts such as entering a building or group interactions.
justifications.overall: Low overall quality due to missing actors, absent vehicles, and several incorrect or omitted semantic labels leading to incomplete scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 6 ground truth pedestrians, missing three other people so recall is about 50 percent.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted attributes cover basic actions and age but missed communicative cues and the pushing transport instance and had some context mismatches, yielding only moderate semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided plausible spatial relations that align with the described sidewalk, shops and construction cones, though ground truth lacks explicit spatial labels for full verification.
justifications.overall: Partial detection and missing behavioral attributes reduce overall reliability despite useful spatial cues, producing a moderate overall assessment.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists four pedestrians matching the count but omits the three parked vehicles and leaves out several ground-truth attributes such as communicative labels and specific transporting types for multiple people.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes clothing cues and actions but misses important semantics like talking in group, talking on phone, correct transporting verbs (pushing versus pulling versus carrying), and any vehicle information.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some local relations to a handcart and an ego-relative relation are provided, but spatial coverage is inconsistent and key scene elements like parked vehicles are not represented.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures basic human presence and some relations but contains several semantic errors and omissions (notably vehicles and communicative/transporting details), resulting in limited utility for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures five pedestrians matching the ground truth count and many per-person attributes but omits all vehicles and misses important communicative labels while mislabeling one transporting action.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes scene context, ages, atomic actions, simple contexts, and transporting labels, but it lacks communicative cues present in the ground truth and contains at least one incorrect transporting assignment.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides explicit spatial relations for several pedestrians which is useful, but these relations cannot be fully validated against the provided ground truth that contains no spatial annotations and some relations appear speculative.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially useful with correct pedestrian counts and many attributes, but missing vehicles, absent communicative labels, and some misassignments reduce its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Partial: the model detected three pedestrians but missed one ground-truth person and introduced a spurious vehicle, so several entities are missing or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Moderate: the extraction supplies many semantic attributes like transporting, communicative, and age, but several attribute values are incorrect or omitted compared to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Good: relations like next to and near stacked boxes are provided and likely accurate, but some relations are missing and there is no alignment to ground-truth ids.
justifications.overall: Fair: the extraction contains useful semantic detail but has missing and incorrect entities and attribute mismatches, reducing its reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 pedestrians and no vehicles compared to 5 persons and 7 vehicles in the ground truth, so a majority of objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a range of pedestrian attributes (actions, transporting, communicative, age) for detected people but omits vehicle attributes entirely and misses some communicative/contextual labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some relative placements but several pedestrians are labeled as 'along the side' versus ground truth 'on the road' and no vehicle spatial info is provided, reducing spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: Missing all vehicles and some pedestrians while partially correct on several person attributes results in a below-average overall extraction quality.

justifications.completeness: All ground truth persons and the parked vehicle are represented in the extraction, though some attributes are incorrect and there are extra false positives.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies many semantic fields beyond the ground truth, but several key labels such as atomic actions and ages are incorrect which limits semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are detailed and generally plausible for the detected agents, but some relations pertain to false positive detections and are not validated by the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Good coverage and rich semantic annotations, but inaccurate attribute labels and additional false positives reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 6 of 8 ground-truth objects (1 person and 5 of 7 vehicles), missing two vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (action, age, vehicle types, motion, trunk/door states) but includes incorrect assertions such as an open rear door and some omitted GT fields.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives several spatial relations that are plausible but sometimes imprecise or inconsistent with the ground-truth context, e.g. pedestrian labeled on the road versus walking along the side.
justifications.overall: Reasonable coverage and rich labeling but errors in vehicle door/trunk status and missing vehicles reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians but failed to capture key ground-truth attributes (communicative and correct transporting) and added a false vehicle, so many GT labels are missing or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many descriptive attributes (clothing hints, actions, spatial notes) which add richness, but several semantic labels are incorrect or extraneous (wrong transporting labels and a false-positive vehicle).
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several positional relations (next to boxes, left side of road), but these are partially unverified or inconsistent with the ground truth and tied to a spurious vehicle.
justifications.overall: Offers useful details and some correct actions, but significant omissions, incorrect transport/communication labels, and a false-positive vehicle materially reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Extraction found the GT person and vehicle but introduced an extra pedestrian and mislabelled the person's action and transporting verb, so only partially complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction supplies many semantic attributes (age, atomic_action, transporting, doors/trunk, spatial_relations) beyond the GT, even though several attribute labels are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations generally match the scene (person and truck near boxes/handcart, other person near barrier) but the extra pedestrian reduces precision.
justifications.overall: Moderately good: correct entity detections and rich semantics are offset by an extra false positive and multiple mislabels (walking vs standing, pulling vs pushing).

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports 5 vehicles versus 7 in the ground truth and omits the single pedestrian, so a substantial portion of objects is missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes many semantic labels (type, motion status, trunk/doors, spatial relations) but also asserts incorrect or unsupported attributes (e.g., an open trunk) and fails to capture some ground-truth fields.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial relations are specific and generally plausible relative to the described scene, but they cannot be fully validated against the provided ground-truth annotations.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is moderately informative with useful semantic detail but is undermined by missing objects and some incorrect attribute estimations.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly captures the single pedestrian but reports only 5 vehicles compared to 7 in the ground truth, so several vehicles are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains richer attributes (vehicle types, trunk/door states, track hints, spatial relations) beyond the ground truth, though there are minor inaccuracies (e.g., an asserted open trunk) and no explicit mapping to GT ids.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides clear left/right/inside/behind relations that align with the scene description and are useful for localization, but some relations cannot be verified for the missing vehicles.
justifications.overall: The extraction is informative and mostly accurate for the pedestrian and many vehicles, but it omits multiple vehicles and contains small semantic inaccuracies, reducing overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures the three vehicles and two pedestrians but misses the pedestrians' true atomic actions and one contextual action (bending/closing) and provides uncertain vehicle attribute details.
justifications.semantic_richness: While vehicle types and visual details like logos are noted, the extraction lacks important semantic labels for people such as walking versus standing and the closing action, reducing semantic depth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Vehicle spatial relations are correct and pedestrian locations are roughly indicated, though pedestrian relations do not reflect the ground truth contextual relation of walking along the side.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: vehicles are well detected and localized but key human actions and context are missed, leading to limited usefulness for behavior interpretation.

justifications.completeness: The extraction includes the single ground-truth pedestrian and four of six annotated vehicles, but misses multiple vehicles and adds two extra pedestrians.
justifications.semantic_richness: The model reports useful attributes (actions, ages, motion status, trunk/door states) for most detections but mislabels a van as a truck and incorrectly marks one vehicle as stopped.
justifications.spatial_relations: Many spatial relations are provided and plausible, but they are informal and cannot be fully verified against the ground truth which lacks relation annotations.
justifications.overall: Solid coverage of key semantic attributes and some spatial info but reduced by missing vehicles, false positive pedestrians, and a few incorrect labels and motion states.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of two persons and one of two vehicles with correct attributes but missed the second pedestrian and the second vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides rich attributes and context for detected objects including actions, age, motion and visual hints, but omits semantics for the missing objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations for the detected pedestrian and truck are correct and informative, but relations for the absent objects are missing and some relations are imprecise.
justifications.overall: Good attribute-level accuracy for the objects it found but moderate completeness and missing entities reduce overall reliability for full scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two persons and one of two vehicles, so significant entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided several semantic attributes including actions and age and even a logo, but missed attributes and the second person's actions, limiting completeness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Accurately states relations for the detected pedestrian and truck, but omits relations for the missing person and vehicle.
justifications.overall: Extraction is partially accurate and semantically informative for detected objects but misses key entities leading to moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detects the walking pedestrian and parked bicycle but misses the second person who is bending and closing, so coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (age, actions, context) but fails to identify the second person's bending/closing action and mislabels some contextual details.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for both agents but contains inaccuracies (claims the pedestrian is in the center rather than along the side) so partially correct.
justifications.overall: Partial and attribute-rich extraction but missing one person and with some spatial inaccuracies, yielding moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The model found two pedestrians matching the ground-truth count but misclassified one person's action (bending/closing vs walking) and incorrectly labeled transporting for the other, omitting correct action/context information.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides additional descriptive details (clothing, cart, spatial relations) beyond the ground truth but includes factual errors in atomic action and transporting labels that reduce reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are detailed and plausible (near left/right building, in front of cart) even though the ground truth does not provide explicit spatial annotations for full verification.
justifications.overall: Overall the VLM produces a reasonably rich and mostly complete detection set but makes important semantic errors (wrong action and transporting) that limit its accuracy for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians but missed the parked vehicle and failed to capture one person's simple context, so coverage is incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes and scene description but includes incorrect transporting and a wrong simple context for one person, reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives specific spatial relations that are plausible and helpful but are not grounded by the ground truth and may include unverified details.
justifications.overall: Overall useful and partly accurate for pedestrians with good detail, but missing the vehicle and containing some incorrect labels, so performance is moderate.

justifications.completeness: Extraction lists both pedestrians and the parked vehicle so entities are mostly complete, but it omits/corrupts the bending person’s 'closing' simple context and adds inappropriate trunk/door fields for the bicycle.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains many rich attributes (scene context, track hints, ages, spatial relations) beyond the ground truth but includes some incorrect or inapplicable labels (missing simple_context for the bender and trunk/door states for a bicycle).
justifications.spatial_relations: Most spatial relations are plausible (bicycle left, distant pedestrian) but the bending person’s relation to a blue cart conflicts with the ground-truth 'closing' context, reducing spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures the main entities and many useful attributes but specific semantic errors (incorrect simple_context and vehicle door/trunk info) lower its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Covers the pedestrian details well but omits the parked vehicle present in the ground truth, so it is incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed pedestrian attributes and scene description but misses vehicle information and object IDs, limiting overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives accurate and useful spatial relations for the pedestrian that align with the scene context.
justifications.overall: Accurate and informative about the person but incomplete due to the missing vehicle, yielding a moderately high overall score.

justifications.completeness: Captured the pedestrian with correct attributes but omitted the parked vehicle from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides extra descriptive context (restaurant awning, alleyway) and detailed attribute labels beyond the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a relation linking the person to the restaurant but fails to report relations to the parked vehicle it omitted.
justifications.overall: Accurate and semantically rich for the detected person, but the missing vehicle reduces the overall correctness and completeness.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses the ground-truth labeling of two seniors who are both sitting (it reports three pedestrians with different actions and ages) and misrepresents the single vehicle, so many ground-truth items are not recovered.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction contains many semantic attributes (actions, ages, spatial relations) showing richness, but several key attributes are incorrect (ages labeled adult instead of senior, wrong actions and vehicle motion), lowering its value.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for detections, but they cannot be confirmed by the ground truth and are tied to misclassified entities (e.g., vehicle described as parked while GT shows it moving), reducing trust in their accuracy.
justifications.overall: Overall the output is descriptive and semantically rich but contains multiple critical errors in pedestrian count, actions, ages, and vehicle motion, making it only modestly useful.

justifications.completeness: Partial: the extraction detects two pedestrians and a vehicle but misses that both pedestrians are seniors and that the second is sitting/biking, and it mislabels the vehicle motion.
justifications.semantic_richness: Moderate: many attributes and relations are provided, but several key semantic labels (age, second pedestrian action, vehicle motion) are incorrect or contradictory with ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Good: the reported spatial relations are specific and plausible given the scene and align well with the described layout even though ground truth lacks explicit spatial annotations.
justifications.overall: Below average: the extraction captures basic entities and some details but contains important semantic errors that reduce its reliability for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: Detects both people and the bicycle and notes the underpass, but misses key labels (e.g., atomic action for the cyclist) and misreports ages (adult vs ground truth senior over 65).
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attribute fields but populates several with 'none of the above' and gives coarse or incorrect values (age, atomic actions), reducing useful semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides accurate and relevant spatial relations (under the underpass, next to) for both pedestrians and the bicycle that match the scene context.
justifications.overall: Overall reasonable entity and spatial detection but notable errors in actions and age labels and overuse of 'none of the above' limit reliability for downstream tasks.

justifications.completeness: Most entities and many attributes are reported but key labels are missing or mismatched such as ages, a missing atomic action for the bicycling person, and incorrect vehicle motion.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides a broad set of semantic attributes and spatial relations beyond the ground truth annotations, showing high descriptive richness despite some incorrect values.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are supplied for both pedestrians and the vehicle and seem plausible, but they cannot be fully validated against the ground truth and may be imprecise.
justifications.overall: Informative and semantically rich overall but several important errors in actions, ages, and vehicle motion reduce reliability for safety-critical use.

justifications.completeness: Identifies the cyclist, bicycle, and scene but misses the correct atomic action and age, omits track IDs, and asserts vehicle door/trunk states not provided in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes and contextual details such as scene description and transport relation, but includes incorrect age, uses many default none labels, and makes unsupported vehicle state claims.
justifications.spatial_relations: Accurately captures spatial relations like on a bicycle and inside an underpass and correctly links the rider to the bicycle.
justifications.overall: Good coverage and spatial detail but mistakes in atomic action, age classification, and unwarranted vehicle state assertions reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians and the bicycle and motion status but missed or misclassified key attributes such as correct ages and one atomic action and incorrectly filled vehicle trunk/door fields.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided many attribute fields and scene description covering actions, context, and communicative states even though some attribute values were incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Captured correct proximity relations between the two persons and correctly linked the bicycle to its rider.
justifications.overall: Good detection and relational extraction but degraded by incorrect attribute values for age, some atomic actions, and inappropriate vehicle trunk/door annotations.

justifications.completeness: Misses correct atomic actions and ages for both persons and adds incorrect transporting info, though detects both people and the bicycle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes and relations beyond the ground truth (transporting, communicative, scene description), but several attributes are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes reasonable spatial relations (inside underpass, next-to) that align with the scene, though ground truth lacks explicit spatial labels for verification.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures key entities and many attributes but has notable errors in actions, age, and transporting leading to moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Most annotated person attributes are present, but the extraction omits an explicit complex contextual field and mislabels age compared to ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction adds useful descriptive detail such as clothing and context but contains an incorrect age classification which reduces semantic correctness.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial relation stating the person is sitting against the wall on the right of the underpass accurately matches the scene description.
justifications.overall: Strong coverage and helpful details are offset by an incorrect age label and a missing explicit complex contextual tag, yielding good but not perfect performance.

justifications.completeness: Captured the single person, action, and absence of vehicles but misclassified age (senior as adult), so it is not fully complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides additional scene context and clothing detail beyond the ground truth which is valuable, but the incorrect age label reduces semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Specifies a detailed right-side wall relation, but the ground truth contains no spatial labels so the claim cannot be confirmed.
justifications.overall: Accurate on presence and action and offers useful extra context, but the age error and unverifiable spatial claim lower the overall quality.
